subject: Computer Networking
title: The Cold War and Networking
description: Learn The Cold War and Networking in one minute.

hook.text: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik in 1957. The U.S. panicked and created ARPA — the internet's birthplace.

buildup.text: ARPA funded advanced research, including ways to connect distant computers for collaboration and resilience.

discovery.text: The military wanted a command network with no single point of failure. A web of nodes, not a central hub.

twist.text: The internet wasn't designed for email or social media. It was designed to survive a nuclear attack.

climax.text: Cold War fear funded the research. But the technology it created connected the entire civilian world.

punchline.text: The internet was born from military fear, not commercial ambition.

quiz.question: What event led to the creation of ARPA?
options: The Soviet launch of Sputnik in 1957
